Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

§ 112 2026.013 KS Market-Based Interest Rate Surcharges for 2027

Description of the Matter The Municipal Council needs to determine the market-based interest rate surcharges for the municipal companies for the year 2027.

The "Financial Guidelines for Alingsås Municipality," adopted by the Municipal Board on December 13, 2021, § 208, states that the Municipal Council annually determines the market-based surcharge for each company. The surcharges applicable for the year 2026 were decided by the Municipal Council on June 11, 2025 (KF 2025-06-11 § 123). This decision needs to be revised for the year 2027.

Preparation The Municipal Administration Office provided the following statement in a letter dated April 7, 2026: All borrowing within the Alingsås municipal group is coordinated centrally, with funds being lent on to the municipal companies. When the municipality issues loans to the group companies, interest rate surcharges are required, which are added to the municipality's own borrowing costs, because it is not permissible to give municipally owned companies a competitive advantage. This means that the companies' financing terms must be market-based.

The market-based interest rate surcharges for 2027 have been analyzed by the municipality's financial advisors. The analysis shows that the interest rate surcharges for AB Alingsås Rådhus and Alingsås Energi AB need to be increased (by 0.01 and 0.06 percentage points, respectively), while the interest rate surcharges for Alingsåshem AB and Alingsåshem lokaler AB need to be reduced (by 0.03 and 0.01 percentage points, respectively).

The Municipal Board addressed this matter on May 11, 2026, § 77.

Decision

  1. The market-based interest rate surcharge for AB Alingsås Rådhus is set at 0.46 percent for the year 2027.
  2. The market-based interest rate surcharge for Alingsåshem AB is set at 0.50 percent for the year 2027.
  3. The market-based interest rate surcharge for Alingsåshem lokaler AB is set at 0.49 percent for the year 2027.
  4. The market-based interest rate surcharge for Alingsås Energi AB is set at 0.39 percent for the year 2027.

5.

Municipal Council Minutes

June 10, 2026

Extract Confirmation

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

§ 113 2026.212 KS Annual Report 2025 for Tolkförmedling Väst Description of the Matter Tolkförmedling Väst has prepared an annual report for the year 2025. The municipal council's elected auditors have reviewed the activities carried out within the municipal association and, in a letter dated 2026-04-08, have approved that the respective municipal councils grant a release from liability to the board and to the individual members of the board.

Preparation The Municipal Management Office provided the following statement in a letter dated April 20, 2026:

The Municipal Management Office has reviewed the annual report for 2025 of the Tolkförmedling Väst association, along with the supporting documentation.

In 2025, the association achieved a pre-tax profit of 17.3 million SEK. Of this, 17 million SEK will be paid to the members, in accordance with the board's decision in November. Alingsås municipality's share of the reimbursement is 0.1 million SEK and was received on 2026-03-31.

The final result is 0.3 million SEK, of which 0.2 million SEK represents the restoration of a negative result from 2023.

During 2025, Alingsås municipality purchased services from Tolkförmedling Väst for approximately 1.8 million SEK, compared to 1.7 million SEK in 2024.

The Municipal Board addressed this matter on May 11, 2026, in item § 78.

Decision

  1. The annual report for 2025 for Tolkförmedling Väst is approved.
  2. A release from liability is granted to the board and to the individual members of the board.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

§ 114 2025.374 KS Revision of the regulations for compensation to elected officials for the period 2027–2030.

Description of the matter: Before each new term, a review of compensation for elected officials is carried out. At the beginning of 2026, the group leaders of the parties in the municipal council have been in dialogue about the compensation levels for the term 2027–2030.

Preparation: The Municipal Management Office provided the following statement in a letter dated April 20, 2026:

The Municipal Management Office has assisted the group leaders with an analysis of compensation for elected officials in other municipalities. The analysis shows that Alingsås has lower fixed compensation levels than municipalities with a comparable population.

The regulations for compensation to elected officials for the period 2027–2030 will be divided into two separate governing documents for the upcoming term. The regulations for compensation to elected officials 2027–2030 will cover compensation and fees, while pensions, transition support, and family protection for elected officials will be addressed in a separate governing document. This will make the regulations for compensation and fees more clear and easier to use in daily work. Issues related to pensions, transition support, and family protection are often more complex and are partly governed by recommendations from SKR (Swedish Association of Local Authorities and Regions). A separate document makes it easier to revise and update these issues as needed.

The following changes are proposed in the regulations:

  1. It is proposed that municipal councilors receive a fee equivalent to 100 percent of the established parliamentary fee. The current level is 95 percent of the parliamentary fee. All fixed fees are increased because they are calculated based on the municipal councilor's fee. The meeting fee is calculated in the same way and is therefore increased from 271 Swedish kronor to 285 Swedish kronor per hour.
  2. It is proposed that additions and adjustments to fixed fees be made in accordance with Appendix 3. The percentages for chairpersons and vice-chairpersons in larger committees are proposed to be increased.
  3. It is proposed to introduce a fixed fee for group leaders of ten percent. A new Appendix 5 is proposed to be introduced to clarify the group leaders' duties and responsibilities.
  4. The preparation fee in the Municipal Board's working committee is to be abolished. The fixed fee for members in the Municipal Board's working committee is proposed to be reduced from 15 percent to 7.5 percent. No fixed fee is paid for members who also receive a group leader fee for assignments in the Municipal Board's working committee.
  5. Members of the Social Welfare Committee's working committee should receive three times the hourly fee for the first hour of meetings that deal with individual cases. Substitute members do not participate in these meetings. This proposal is motivated by the fact that the meeting documents are often extensive, while the meetings are usually relatively short. The higher fee is intended to compensate for the time required to read and familiarize oneself with the documents.
  6. Compensation for lost income is paid at the actual lost income, but not exceeding an hourly amount corresponding to 0.6 percent of the Municipal Board's chairperson's 9

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

Monthly allowance: The municipality will compensate for actual loss of earnings for individuals with a monthly salary up to the monthly allowance of the chair of the municipal board, which in 2026 corresponds to approximately 81,400 Swedish kronor per month. This proposal represents an increase compared to the current regulations.

  1. Compensation for auditors is proposed to follow the same compensation model as for members. This means that compensation is only paid for board positions within AB Alingsås Rådhus, and for one board position each within the property group and the energy group. Compensation is therefore not paid for each individual company within the groups.

The proposed changes and additions are highlighted in yellow in the regulations for compensation to elected officials. Text marked in red, concerning pensions, transition support, and family protection, will be removed from the regulations and treated in a separate policy document. Text that is crossed out will be removed.

On May 11, 2026, in item 79, the municipal board considered this matter and proposed that the "Regulations for Compensation to Elected Officials" for the years 2027–2030 be approved.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Sanna Dabolins (V), Karin Schagerlind (M), and Marcus Wallin (V).

Proposed resolutions for the meeting Sanna Dabolins (V) proposes the following changes to the regulations for compensation to elected officials:

  1. The allowance for the chair of the municipal board is set at 70 percent of the parliamentary allowance, and the allowance for the vice-chair of the municipal board is set at 60 percent of the parliamentary allowance.
  2. All allowances should be calculated based on 100 percent of the parliamentary allowance, instead of currently being based on the chair of the municipal board's allowance, in order to maintain the increased compensation level for other allowances.
  3. Furthermore, we request that all relevant parts of the regulations be revised in accordance with this principle.

Karin Schagerlind (M) proposes the following change to the regulations for compensation to elected officials: In the "Regulations for Compensation to Elected Officials" 2027–2030, Appendix 2, "Allowances for Elected Officials," the third paragraph after the table on page 20 should be changed to the following: The allowance for the board of directors of the Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association should follow the Alingsås municipality's regulations for allowances and compensation when the association's board of directors makes decisions about compensation according to the association's bylaws.

Decision-making process The chair will first put the municipal board's proposed resolution against Sanna Dabolins' proposed amendment and will determine that the municipal council will decide according to the municipal board's proposal.

Voting A vote is requested. 10

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

The following voting procedure is approved: Yes vote in favor of the proposal from the Municipal Board. No vote in favor of the proposal from Sanna Dabolin.

Voting Results With 38 yes votes, 10 no votes, and 1 abstention, the chairperson determines that the Municipal Council resolves to approve the proposal from the Municipal Board. The voting results are at the end of this section.

The chairperson then put Karin Schagerlind's amendment to the proposal from the Municipal Board to a vote, and the Municipal Council voted in favor of Karin Schagerlind's amendment.

Decision The regulations for compensation to elected officials for the period 2027–2030 are adopted, with the following amendment: In the regulations for compensation to elected officials for the period 2027–2030, Appendix 2, "Fees for Elected Officials," the third paragraph after the table on page 20 is amended to read as follows: "The compensation for the board of directors of the Alingsås and Vårgårda Fire and Rescue Service Association should follow the compensation and allowance regulations of the Alingsås municipality when the association's board of directors makes decisions regarding compensation according to the association's bylaws."

Dissenting Opinion Sanna Dabolin (V), Martin Lindberg (V), Christoffer Salander (V), Maria Stern (V), and Marcus Wallin (V) dissent and support their own proposal.

To be sent to: Municipal Administration Office

Supporting Documents • §79 Municipal Board: Revision of the regulations for compensation to elected officials for the years 2027–2030. • Service memo: Revision of the regulations for compensation to elected officials 2027-2030. • Regulations for compensation to elected officials for the years 2027-2030.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

§ 115 2026.225 KS Introduction of General Political Debates in the Municipal Council Description of the Matter On December 10, 2025, the Municipal Council, in § 246, decided to approve the motion to introduce general political debates in the Municipal Council, proposed by Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C), Annika Qarlsson (C), Ingbritt Johansson (C), Ali Said (C), and Theresa Montebelli (C).

The Municipal Council further decided to task the Municipal Council's Presidium with developing a proposal regarding the format and implementation of general political debates during Municipal Council meetings, and that the agenda should be reviewed and revised before the new term.

Subsequently, the Municipal Council's Presidium researched how general political debates are structured in other municipalities. A routine for general political debates has been developed and is attached as supporting documentation. Group leaders have been given the opportunity to provide comments on the routine.

General political debates should, as a general rule, be held twice a year, in accordance with the intention of the motion. When implementing these debates, established timeframes and clear rules for speeches, rebuttals, and participation will apply, as clarified in the routine.

The proposal involves introducing general political debates before the 2027–2030 term and approving the routine for general political debates.

Preparation The Municipal Administration Office, in a letter dated May 8, 2026, provided the following statement:

The routine regulates, among other things, the format of the debate, timeframes, speeches, rebuttals, and participation. The administration believes that the proposed routine creates clear and equitable frameworks for conducting general political debates in the Municipal Council.

The Municipal Board addressed the matter on June 1, 2026, in § 97.

Decision

  1. The routine for general political debates is approved.
  2. General political debates are introduced in the Municipal Council before the 2027–2030 term.

To be sent to: Municipal Administration Office

Municipal Council Minutes

June 10, 2026

Extract Confirmation

Decision-Making Documents:

  • §97: Introduction of general political debates in the municipal council.
  • Service memo: Introduction of general political debates.
  • Proposed procedure for general political debates.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

§ 117 2026.203 KS Granting of discharge for the board of the Coordination Association "Insjöriket" for the fiscal year 2025. Description of the matter: The municipality of Alingsås has received the annual report, audit report, and review report for the Coordination Association "Insjöriket" for the fiscal year 2025. The municipality of Alingsås must consider the issue of granting discharge to the board of the coordination association and its members.

The Coordination Association "Insjöriket" is an independent legal organization with the following members: the Public Employment Service, the Swedish Social Insurance Agency, the Västra Götaland Region, and the municipalities of Mölndal, Partille, Härryda, Lerum, and Alingsås. The Coordination Association "Insjöriket" works to improve cooperation between various authorities that work with individuals who are far from the labor market. The association finances and coordinates initiatives where the municipality, the Swedish Social Insurance Agency, the Public Employment Service, and the Västra Götaland Region cooperate, with the goal of providing individuals with coordinated support tailored to their needs.

Preparation: The Municipal Management Office issued a statement on April 23, 2026, stating the following:

The Municipal Management Office has reviewed the 2025 annual report for the Coordination Association "Insjöriket" and its attachments.

Azets Revision och Rådgivning AB, acting on behalf of the coordination association's elected auditors, has reviewed the association's annual report for 2025 and provided an audit report and a review report of the balance sheet and annual report. The review aims to provide the coordination association's auditors with information for their written assessment of whether the annual report has been prepared in accordance with legal requirements and generally accepted accounting principles, and whether the result is consistent with the goals set by the board.

The elected auditors assess that the board has conducted the operations in a purposeful and economically satisfactory manner, and that the internal controls have been sufficient. The accounts are considered to be essentially fair, and the annual report has been prepared in accordance with the Municipal Accounting Act and generally accepted accounting principles. The elected auditors recommend that the board and its members be granted discharge.

The Municipal Management Office has nothing further to add than what is stated in the annual report and its attachments, and therefore proposes that the municipal council grant discharge to the board and its members of the Coordination Association "Insjöriket."

The Municipal Board addressed the matter on May 11, 2026, in item § 82.

Decision: The board of the Coordination Association "Insjöriket" is granted discharge for the fiscal year 2025.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

To be sent to: Samordningsförbundet Insjöriket

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

§ 118 2025.563 KS Repeal of Alingsås Municipality's Competition Policy Description of the Matter The Municipal Board's working committee decided on November 12, 2025, § 199, to assign the municipal administration office the task of reviewing and revising the municipality's process and procedures for the right of challenge.

This review and revision is considered necessary because the current governing documents are outdated. The Municipal Council decided on November 24, 2010, § 191, to introduce the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality from April 1, 2011, and the Municipal Board adopted the application guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality on November 26, 2012, § 193.

The Municipal Council decided on March 23, 2011, § 38, to adopt a revised competition policy. This competition policy is still in effect and has not been revised since then.

The Municipal Board's working committee decided on January 21, 2026, § 20, to refer this matter back for further consideration.

Preparation In a letter dated April 20, 2026, the municipal administration office provided the following statement:

The municipal administration office believes that the current governing document, the Competition Policy, is superfluous and has no value because there is a separate decision stating that Alingsås Municipality has introduced the right of challenge, and there are adopted application guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality. The proposal from the municipal administration office is to have one governing document for the right of challenge instead of two. Therefore, it is proposed that the current application guidelines be repealed by the Municipal Board, and that the Municipal Board adopt guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality.

The municipal administration office has therefore prepared a draft of guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality, which are based on the current application guidelines. The basis of the right of challenge is the possibility of expressing the opinion to the municipality that an activity carried out by the municipality could be performed more efficiently by someone else. It is whether the activity itself should be subject to competition through procurement that should be investigated, not whether the party who has submitted the opinion is a suitable supplier. Therefore, it should not be necessary to require extensive documentation from the person who wants to submit a challenge to the municipality, but the focus should be on the internal municipal process of investigating whether the activity should continue to be carried out internally or whether it should be subject to competition through procurement.

After being referred back by the Municipal Board's working committee, the draft guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality have higher requirements for the challenger, requiring them to provide references from similar activities within municipal operations, and that the challenger is a serious candidate to participate in a potential future procurement process.

The municipal administration office proposes that the Municipal Board repeal the application guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality and that the guidelines for the right of challenge in Alingsås Municipality be adopted.

18

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

The municipal administration proposes that the municipal council repeal the competition policy.

On April 29, 2026, in item 88, the municipal board considered the matter and decided, for its part, to abolish the regulations for challenges in the municipality of Alingsås, to adopt the guidelines for the right to challenge in the municipality of Alingsås, and to propose to the municipal council that the competition policy be repealed.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Anton César (S), Sanna Dabolins (V), Mats Eriksson (MP), Jan-Erik Wallin (M), Marcus Wallin (V), and Lady France Mulumba (KD).

Proposed Resolution for the Meeting Jan-Erik Wallin (M) and Lady France Mulumba (KD) propose that the municipal board's proposed resolution be approved.

Resolution The competition policy is repealed.

Did Not Participate in the Decision Sebastian Aronsson (S), Birgit Börjesson (S), Anton César (S), Morgan Fredvi (S), Leif Hansson (S), Irene Jonsson (S), Pamela Nilsson Ludvigsson (S), Peter Norlander (S), Håkan Stern (S), Luzaan Eisa (S), Jenny Helgesson (S), Britt Andersson Näsman (S), Oscar Allard (S), and Alexander Wikhall (S) did not participate in the decision.

Sanna Dabolins (V), Martin Lindberg (V), Christoffer Salander (V), Maria Stern (V), and Marcus Wallin (V) did not participate in the decision.

Mats Eriksson (MP) and Ulla Dalén (MP) did not participate in the decision.

Note Sebastian Aronsson (S), Birgit Börjesson (S), Anton César (S), Morgan Fredvi (S), Leif Hansson (S), Irene Jonsson (S), Pamela Nilsson Ludvigsson (S), Peter Norlander (S), Håkan Stern (S), Luzaan Eisa (S), Jenny Helgesson (S), Britt Andersson Näsman (S), Oscar Allard (S), and Alexander Wikhall (S) made the following note in the minutes: The Social Democrats did not participate in the decision because we fundamentally oppose the politically introduced right to challenge in the municipality of Alingsås. The right to challenge is not legally mandated but a voluntary system that the municipality itself has chosen to implement. For us, it is clear that this is part of an ideologically driven privatization drive, where municipal activities are increasingly subject to market experiments. We believe that the handling of incoming challenges places a burden on the municipal organization through extensive administration, investigative work, and resource consumption. Ironically, those who defend the right to challenge often refer to efficiency improvements and reduced administration as overarching goals. In practice, the right to challenge risks creating the opposite, where time and resources are taken from core activities to manage processes whose main purpose is to test...

Municipal Council Minutes

June 10, 2026

Confirmation of Extract

Regarding privatization: The municipality's focus should instead be on developing the quality of its own operations and creating long-term conditions for social welfare, rather than pursuing recurring organizational experiments involving outsourcing and privatization.

Sent to: Municipal Management Office (finance)

Supporting Documents:

  • §88: Review and revision of Alingsås Municipality's process and procedures for the right of challenge.
  • Competition Policy for Alingsås Municipality.

20

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

§ 119 2025.319 KS Response to motion regarding support for associations that organize night patrols - Irene Jonsson (S) and others.

Description of the matter: Irene Jonsson (S), Sebastian Aronsson (S), Camilla Stensson (S), and Peter Norlander (S) submitted a motion to the municipal council on June 11, 2025, regarding support for associations that organize night patrols.

The proposers suggest the following:

  • To introduce increased financial support for associations that organize night patrols, particularly in areas and at times where the need is greatest.
  • That the municipality, in collaboration with civil society, develops a long-term plan to coordinate, support, and strengthen preventative safety initiatives – where parents and other adults are encouraged to participate in night patrols together with associations and networks.

On June 11, 2025, the municipal council decided in § 144 to refer the motion to the municipal board for consideration.

Preparation: The municipal administration office provided the following statement in a letter dated May 13, 2026:

Currently, there is no organized night patrol program in Alingsås that is funded by the municipality. The issue of organized night patrols has been discussed in a new working group focused on reducing risk factors for crime among children and young people, and it could be a way and a new tool to work with.

Adult presence can increase people's sense of safety in a place and also be a crime prevention measure. Night patrols are carried out daily in several municipalities near Alingsås, with varying scope and funding. What is common for most municipalities is that the cooperation between municipalities and associations regarding night patrols is increasing.

In Borås Municipality, they operate a program called "Adult Patrols," where the city, together with the police, works preventatively in connection with various holidays and events such as school graduations, school openings, and Walpurgis Night. By having parents and other adults present in the evenings where young people gather, the risk of young people getting into trouble is reduced.

Within the municipality, a new way of collaborating on issues related to children and young people was initiated in 2025, based on the new Social Services Act (2025:400), which came into force on July 1, 2025. A new working group focused on children and young people was established – to reduce risk factors for crime among children and young people – and this is part of the municipality's crime prevention cooperation.

The working group focused on reducing risk factors for crime among children and young people is working to analyze needs and ongoing initiatives in the municipality. There are different initiatives and different needs in different parts of the municipality. The working group wants to review the overall picture before introducing a new tool.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

Regarding the establishment and administration of programs, adult night patrols could be a part of supporting and strengthening preventative safety measures, and a new tool that can be implemented.

The working group focused on reducing risk factors for crime among children and young people, and in dialogue with the police, has found that there is a perceived high level of safety in the municipality, and that there is well-functioning cooperation regarding youth groups that are active outdoors. There is already established cooperation for events, such as the potato festival, where the culture and education department implements safety measures, and where associations and young people can participate in keeping the municipality clean and tidy. Furthermore, the local police department has initiatives with police volunteers at various events.

The working group focused on reducing risk factors for crime among children and young people is working on developing a plan to expand safety measures for children and young people, and wants to have data to analyze which measures are effective and to be able to measure this. The working group currently sees that we need to focus on preventing social exclusion. This is something that both associations and civil society can contribute to and receive support for.

The municipal management office therefore believes that the motion should be rejected in its entirety at this stage, and that the working group focused on reducing risk factors for crime among children and young people should return with proposals for measures.

The Municipal Board addressed the matter on June 1, 2026, in item 101.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Peter Norlander (S), Adam Jansson (KD), and Marcus Wallin (V).

Proposal for a decision at the meeting Adam Jansson (KD) proposes that the Municipal Board's proposed decision be approved.

Decision The motion is considered to have been answered.

§ 120 2026.126 KS Response to motion regarding strengthening the association life in Alingsås by connecting to the Grant Portal - Anders Julin (S) and others. Description of the matter: Anders Julin (S), Alexander Wikhall (S), and Sebastian Aronsson (S) submitted a motion to the municipal council on February 25, 2026, regarding strengthening the association life in Alingsås municipality by connecting to the Grant Portal.

The proposers suggest the following:

  • Alingsås municipality investigates the possibility and cost of connecting to the Grant Portal.

On February 25, 2025, the municipal council, in § 47, decided to refer the motion to the municipal board for preparation. The Culture and Education Committee received the motion on March 3, 2026, for further preparation and response before June 3, 2026.

Preparation: The municipal administration, in a letter dated May 11, 2026, provided the following statement:

The Culture and Education Committee considered the matter on April 22, 2026, in § 48, and the committee supports the motion. The Culture and Education Department has investigated the Grant Portal, which is a combined monitoring, process, and project tool for finding, applying for, and using grants, allowing municipalities and organizations to connect. It enables the municipality's associations to find available grants for associations throughout the country in one place. During the Culture and Education Committee's meetings with associations, it has become clear that there is a general need for associations to receive clear information about which grants are available. The Grant Portal could be a useful tool to meet the needs of the associations. The Grant Portal is free for associations when the municipality is connected. The Culture and Education Department believes that it is possible, within the budget framework, to connect to the Grant Portal for a trial period of 1 year at a monthly cost of 5,400 SEK/month. The Culture and Education Department sees no need for further investigation and proposes that the investigation be considered completed and that the motion be approved.

The municipal administration therefore believes that the motion should be approved in accordance with the Culture and Education Committee's proposal.

The municipal board considered the matter on June 1, 2026, in § 100.

Speeches: Speeches will be given by Alexander Wikhall (S) and Holger Andersson (L).

Proposal for a decision at the meeting: Alexander Wikhall (S) proposes that the municipal board's proposed decision be approved.

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

Decision: The motion is approved.

To be sent to: The Culture and Education Department

§ 121 2025.503 KS Response to motion regarding strengthening support for the Ukrainian people - Anton César (S) and Leif Hansson (S) Description of the matter Anton César (S) and Leif Hansson (S) submitted a motion to the municipal council on November 5, 2025, regarding strengthening support for the Ukrainian people.

The proposers suggest the following:

  • Alingsås municipality should participate in the collaboration between the Gothenburg region (GR) and the Lviv region, and offer to host visits during the planned study visit in 2026.
  • The municipal board should be tasked with developing contacts with the Lviv region and seeking a partner municipality that needs support and collaboration.
  • The municipal board should be tasked with investigating how the municipality can collaborate with "Mötesplats Alingsås/Alingsås supports Ukraine" to strengthen support for Ukrainian refugees locally.

On November 5, 2025, the municipal council decided in § 224 to refer the motion to the municipal board for preparation.

Preparation The municipal administration office provided the following statement in a letter dated May 11, 2026:

Alingsås municipality has in various ways shown support for the Ukrainian people and has supported Ukrainian refugees who have fled to Alingsås under the mass displacement directive from Ukraine. This means that Ukrainian citizens and their families who have fled have the right to work, healthcare, education, and financial assistance. The directive has been extended several times and is valid until March 4, 2027. Ukrainian citizens can now also register in Sweden after having had a residence permit under the mass displacement directive for one year.

The Swedish Association of Local Authorities and Regions (SKR), together with the Gothenburg region (GR) and other municipal associations, has initiated a collaboration with Ukraine. This has led to a study visit to the Lviv region, which took place at the end of March. The municipal councilor and deputy chairman of the municipal board of Alingsås municipality participated in this study visit. In this collaboration, Alingsås municipality has been assigned Brirka, a municipality in the Lviv area. During the study visit, discussions were held about potential areas of collaboration that need to be explored. These include civil preparedness, electricity and water supply, and the restoration of ruins. There may be opportunities to apply for EU funding and collaborations that strengthen both Brirka and Alingsås municipality. Based on the fact that a collaboration has been initiated within SKR and GR, Alingsås municipality can continue to be part of this collaboration.

With the possibility for Ukrainian refugees to register, they now have the same rights to support as other refugees with residence permits. The reception of Ukrainians under the mass displacement directive continues to decrease and, according to the Swedish Migration Agency's forecasts, is also at relatively low levels for 2026. This means that the social services department will be able to continue to provide the same support to this group within the framework of the reception mandate.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

Regarding the integration of newly arrived individuals, in collaboration with civil society organizations, the employment agency, and the immigration agency. Discussions with the administration indicate that an ongoing collaboration and established structure already exists to support Ukrainian refugees in Alingsås. The majority of Ukrainians residing in Alingsås are registered residents, with only a few not being registered. The social services department already has an established collaboration with "Mötesplats Alingsås/Alingsås Supports Ukraine," both in providing support to individual refugees and to the group as a whole.

The Municipal Management Office emphasizes that the municipality's efforts and contributions must remain within the scope of its municipal responsibilities. This means that the initiatives must be of general interest, proportionate, and objective. Furthermore, the municipality should not handle matters that are solely the responsibility of the state or other entities. If further collaboration regarding refugees is to be explored, it should involve collaboration with all refugees in Alingsås, regardless of the basis on which they are residing there. If necessary, this can be investigated. This is a matter for the social welfare board according to current regulations. It is not possible to create special solutions that only target refugees from a specific region or country. This is not in accordance with current legislation.

One area where collaboration with "Mötesplats Alingsås/Alingsås Supports Ukraine" might be possible is the potential for sending outdated furniture from renovations of business premises to Ukraine, if there is a need and agreement.

Based on the above, the Municipal Management Office believes that the motion can be considered answered.

The Municipal Board addressed this matter on June 1, 2026, in item § 102.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Leif Hansson (S), Holger Andersson (L), Anton César (S), and Jan-Erik Wallin (M).

Decision The motion is considered answered.

Does not participate in the decision It is noted in the minutes that Sanna Dabolin (V) left the meeting before a decision was made. 26

§ 122 2025.490 KS Response to motion regarding defibrillators in municipal vehicles - Kjerstin Hansson (SD)

Description of the matter Kjerstin Hansson (SD) submitted a motion to the municipal council on November 5, 2025, regarding defibrillators in municipal vehicles.

The motion proposes the following: That the municipality equips all vehicles used by the municipality with defibrillators. That staff receive training in the use of defibrillators. That staff receive training in how to act as SMS lifesavers.

On November 5, 2025, the municipal council, in § 226, decided to refer the motion to the municipal board for consideration. The technical committee received the motion from the municipal management office for consideration of the first "that" clause on November 19, 2025. The technical committee addressed the matter on March 23, 2026, in § 17.

Preparation The municipal management office, in a letter dated April 20, 2026, provided the following statement: The technical committee has considered the proposal to equip all vehicles used by the municipality with defibrillators. The municipality's vehicle fleet consists of approximately 220 vehicles, of which about 140 are passenger cars and about 80 are work vehicles. This would require investments, maintenance, and increased administration. There are both advantages and disadvantages to the proposal, and the total estimated investment cost is approximately 10 million Swedish krona. The technical committee also noted that it is not explicitly within the municipality's competence to equip all municipal vehicles with defibrillators, as this could be considered an aid within the framework of emergency medical care according to the health and medical care act. The technical committee also emphasizes that the municipality should cooperate with the region and ensure that the measure is consistent with the requirements of the health and medical care act, and that the municipality must consider practical aspects such as training, insurance, and cost responsibility before making a decision. The technical committee, in § 17 on March 23, 2026, decided to reject the first "that" clause of the motion based on the above reasoning.

The municipal management office shares the assessment of the technical committee. There are both advantages and disadvantages that must be weighed against each other. Approximately 200 vehicles would need to be equipped with defibrillators, and approximately 250-280 people drive municipal vehicles each month. The majority of the municipality's vehicle fleet is parked outdoors, not in garages. This must be taken into account from a safety perspective, as well as regarding operation, storage temperature, and operating limits for defibrillators.

According to the Swedish defibrillator registry, there are approximately 130 defibrillators registered in Alingsås municipality, of which approximately 25 are within the municipality's operations.

It is not possible to estimate how many of the approximately 250-280 people who drive municipal vehicles have current CPR (Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation) training, which is required to use defibrillators and register as SMS lifesavers. A CPR training course is approximately 3.5 hours, and a training effort would require at least 875 hours of work time. Furthermore, 28...

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

The municipal administration office states that it must be voluntary to act as an SMS-lifeguard, and it should not be something that can be mandatory as part of the job. This commitment applies 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

The municipal administration office's assessment is that the motion should be rejected, as there is no justification for equipping all vehicles in the municipality's vehicle fleet with defibrillators, or for requiring all staff who drive municipal vehicles to be trained in using defibrillators and acting as SMS-lifeguards.

The Municipal Board addressed the matter on May 11, 2026, in item 83, and proposed that the motion be rejected in its entirety.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Kjerstin Hansson (SD) and Adam Jansson (KD).

Proposal for a decision at the meeting Kjerstin Hansson (SD) proposes that the motion be approved.

Adam Jansson (KD) proposes that the Municipal Board's proposed decision be approved.

Decision-making process The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the municipal council decides to approve the Municipal Board's proposal.

Decision The motion is rejected in its entirety.

Note Lady France Mulumba (KD), Kent Perciwall (KD), Ann-Lie Tulokas Olsson (KD), and Adam Jansson (KD) submit the following note to the minutes: The motion raises an important issue. The chances of survival after a cardiac arrest depend on how people react in the seconds following the event. The Christian Democrats largely sympathize with the municipal administration office's response to the motion, as it is difficult to require many of the municipality's employees to act as the first point of contact in the care chain as part of their employment without any formal training. However, we believe that the municipality should invest in more defibrillators and ensure that all of the municipality's defibrillators are accessible to the public at all hours of the day. 29

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

§ 123 2025.493 KS Response to a motion regarding the investigation of the possibility of creating more housing by converting attics in Alingsåhem properties - Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C)

Description of the matter Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C) submitted a motion to the municipal council on November 5, 2025, proposing an investigation into the possibility of creating more housing by converting attics in Alingsåhem properties.

The motion proposes that:

  • The municipal council instructs Alingsåhem to inventory its properties to identify those suitable for conversion of attics into housing.
  • The results of the inventory are presented to the municipal council, along with a plan for how the housing stock can be expanded through this possibility.

On November 5, 2025, the municipal council, in item § 228, decided to forward the motion to the municipal board for preparation.

Preparation The Department of Urban Planning submitted the following statement in a letter dated May 13, 2026:

The Planning and Building Act was amended on December 1, 2025, which includes simplifications in the application process for building permits for certain measures. This applies, for example, to the conversion of attics, even if the current zoning plan prohibits such conversions. However, certain conditions must be met for the property owner to be able to convert an attic. The following conditions apply:

  • The building is a residential building.
  • The implementation period of the zoning plan has expired.
  • The deviation, together with other deviations, is not too extensive.
  • The deviation does not restrict any right or ongoing activity in the surrounding area.
  • The deviation does not have a significant environmental impact.

These conditions are assessed when applying for a building permit, and it may be that attic spaces initially considered suitable do not meet all the conditions when applying for a building permit. For the conversion of attics to be approved, the Planning and Building Act, the Planning and Building Ordinance, and the Swedish Board of Housing, Urban Planning and Construction's building regulations must be met. This includes legal requirements for technical properties such as fire safety, accessibility, ceiling height, daylight, etc.

The municipal administration supports further conversion of attics and an increased housing stock, but an inventory of Alingsåhem's properties is needed to determine which...

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

Attics that could be considered suitable for renovation risk being too extensive. To determine whether an attic can be renovated, a project must be carried out to assess the specific building's suitability for attic renovation, and the final assessment of attic renovation is made when applying for a building permit.

The Municipal Administration believes that the motion should be rejected because an inventory will not be able to provide a complete picture of which properties are actually suitable for renovation, and that a more in-depth project is required for each attic.

On June 1, 2026, the Municipal Board addressed the matter in item 99 and proposed that the motion be rejected in its entirety.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C), Thorsten Larsson (M), Jan-Erik Wallin (M), and Anton César (S).

Proposal for a decision at the meeting Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C) proposes that the motion be approved.

Thorsten Larsson (M), Jan-Erik Wallin (M), and Anton César (S) propose that the Municipal Board's proposed decision be approved.

Decision-making process The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the Municipal Council decides to approve the Municipal Board's proposal.

Voting A vote is requested.

The following voting process is approved: Yes vote for approving the Municipal Board's proposal. No vote for approving the motion.

Voting results With 42 yes votes and 6 no votes, the chairperson determines that the Municipal Council decides to approve the Municipal Board's proposal to reject the motion.

The voting results are at the end of this section.

Decision The motion is rejected in its entirety. 32

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Extract Confirmation

Note: Linda Espeving (M), Dennis Alvdén (M), Inger Carlborg (M), Eva-Lotta Pamp (M), Magnus Linde (M), Thomas Thim (M), Karin Schagerlind (M), Thorsten Larsson (M), Per-Gordon Tranberg (M), Jimmy Lanevik (M), Lady France Mulumba (KD), Kent Perciwall (KD), Ann-Lie Tulokas Olsson (KD), Adam Jansson (KD), Holger Andersson (L), Jens Christian Berlin (L) and Karin Josefsson (L) submitted the following note to the minutes: We view positively the new legislation that provides opportunities for more building permits for the conversion of attics in residential buildings. It is Alingsåshem's responsibility to develop its property portfolio, and this includes densification and renovations, such as converting attics into living spaces. We assume that the company will take advantage of the opportunities provided by the new legislation. At the same time, we agree with the administration's assessment that the inventory and subsequent plan proposed in the motion risk being too comprehensive. In this context, we also hope that private property owners will also take advantage of the regulatory change to create more housing in existing buildings.

Sebastian Aronsson (S), Birgit Börjesson (S), Anton César (S), Morgan Fredvi (S), Leif Hansson (S), Irene Jonsson (S), Pamela Nilsson Ludvigsson (S), Peter Norlander (S), Håkan Stern (S), Luzaan Eisa (S), Jenny Helgesson (S), Britt Andersson Näsman (S), Oscar Allard (S) and Alexander Wikhall (S) submitted the following note to the minutes: The Social Democrats would like to emphasize that we are positive about densification, which is in line with both the comprehensive plan and the Alingsås city plan. Creating more housing through the conversion of attics is a good example of sustainable and resource-efficient construction, where existing buildings and infrastructure are used more effectively. Densification also means that more residents can live in areas with access to municipal services, commerce, and public transportation. This contributes to more sustainable urban development and more efficient use of already developed areas. We therefore view it positively that both private property owners and the municipal housing company create attic apartments where it is possible and appropriate. At the same time, we do not believe that a special inventory of Alingsåshem's property portfolio is necessary, given the preparation of the motion. However, the issue is important, and we intend to continue to monitor it both in the municipal board's dialogue with Alingsåshem and within the company's board of directors. Our understanding is that Alingsåshem is already proactively working to create more housing in connection with major renovations and upgrades, including in Stockslycke and Noltorp.

Heidi Hankanen Ängberg (C), Ingbritt Johansson (C), Annika Qarlsson (C) and Johan Fogelberg (C) submitted the following note to the minutes: The Center Party regrets that the municipal council decided to reject the motion to investigate the possibility of creating more housing through the conversion of attics in Alingsåshem's properties. The motion does not aim to determine which attics can ultimately be developed or to replace the design and building permit process that is required. The purpose is to create a knowledge base about the possibilities that may exist within the existing property portfolio through an inventory. The argument that an inventory cannot provide a complete answer is not a reason to reject the motion.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of Extract

Regarding the motion. Conversely, it is precisely through surveys and overall analyses that the municipality normally identifies development opportunities before more detailed investigations are carried out. Given the need for housing and the changes in the Planning and Building Act that facilitate the conversion of attics, the Center Party believes that the municipality and Alingsåshem should have explored the possibility of identifying attics that could be converted. By rejecting the proposal, they are foregoing the opportunity to even investigate what possibilities exist to increase the housing stock in an efficient manner in existing buildings. According to the Center Party, it is surprising that Alingsåshem has not taken, or apparently plans to take, any action based on the changes in the Planning and Building Act that came into force on December 1, 2025. According to the Center Party, it is not sufficient that Alingsåshem, as stated in the debate, only reviews unused spaces that could be converted into housing during major renovations.

§ 124 2025.446 KS Response to motion regarding the introduction of charging stations for cyclists in Alingsås - Amanda Andersson (Green Party) Description of the matter Amanda Andersson (Green Party) submitted a motion to the municipal council on September 24, 2025, § 204, regarding the introduction of charging stations for cyclists in Alingsås.

The motion proposes the following:

  • That the municipal council decides to instruct the Technical Committee, in collaboration with Alingsås Energi, to introduce theft-proof charging stations for electric bicycles in the municipality of Alingsås.
  • That the municipal council urges Alingsåshem (the municipal housing company) to introduce charging stations for electric bicycles in larger residential areas.

The municipal council referred the motion to the Municipal Board for preparation on September 24, § 204.

The Technical Committee received the motion for preparation from the Chairman of the Municipal Board on September 29, 2025.

The Technical Committee decided on December 15, § 65, to reject the motion.

Preparation The Municipal Administration Office provided the following statement in a letter dated May 11, 2026:

The Municipal Administration Office shares the assessment of the Urban Planning Department that the proposal in the motion to introduce charging stations for cyclists in Alingsås is in line with the vision described in the municipality of Alingsås' comprehensive plan, adopted by the municipal council on October 31, 2018, § 182, regarding:

  • That Alingsås should be a cycling city.
  • That the municipality of Alingsås should be part of an efficient communication network where public transport and walking and cycling are prioritized.

The traffic plan for the municipality of Alingsås, adopted by the municipal council on April 3, 2012, § 132, also includes a goal of increasing the proportion of active transportation, which includes cyclists and pedestrians. Furthermore, the proposal is in line with the municipality's climate strategy goals of increased sustainable travel and reduced greenhouse gas emissions.

However, the demand for public charging points for electric bicycles is considered to be low, especially in the center of Alingsås, where cycling distances are short. An electric bicycle with a standard battery (approximately 400 Wh) typically has a range of 50-60 km per charge, and larger batteries can enable a range of up to approximately 150 km. Electric bicycles are usually also equipped with removable batteries that can be charged at home or at work. Therefore, the need for special charging stations for electric bicycles is considered to be limited.

Municipal Council Minutes

2026-06-10

Confirmation of excerpt

The public space is limited, and further investigation is needed regarding how to prevent theft of the removable batteries before public charging stations are established. Additionally, a suitable payment solution for the charging needs to be explored.

The Municipal Administration Office has consulted with Alingsåshem in preparing this matter. Property managers at the company have not observed any demand for special charging stations for electric bicycles from tenants, which suggests that the need is currently limited.

The Municipal Administration Office proposes that both proposals in the motion be rejected.

On June 1, 2026, the Municipal Board addressed this matter in item 103 and proposed that the motion be rejected in its entirety.

Speeches Speeches will be given by Mats Eriksson (MP), Martin Lindberg (V), Jan-Erik Wallin (M), Ingbritt Johansson (C), Anton César (S), and Holger Andersson (L).

Proposed resolutions for the meeting Mats Eriksson (MP), Martin Lindberg (V), and Ingbritt Johansson (C) propose that the motion be approved.

Jan-Erik Wallin (M) proposes that the Municipal Board's proposed resolution be approved.

Decision-making process The chairperson puts the proposals to a vote and determines that the Municipal Council decides to approve the Municipal Board's proposal.

Decision The motion is rejected in its entirety.

tldr: In the municipality of Alingsås, politicians have made important decisions regarding the financial regulations for municipal companies and have awarded the Environmental Prize for 2025. They have also received several proposals (motions) covering a wide range of topics, from new housing developments to bicycle paths.
lattlast:
At the municipal council meeting, the Building and Environment Committee awarded the environmental prize for the year 2027 to the company Vikaryds Egendoms AB. The committee also discussed the interest rate that the municipality should apply when lending money to its own companies in 2027. It is important that the interest rate is market-based, meaning it should be comparable to rates offered by a regular bank, so that the companies do not receive an unfair advantage.

The politicians also received several proposals, known as motions. These include proposals to build more housing by converting attics in Alingsåshem buildings, to install charging stations for cyclists, and to investigate the possibility of building pedestrian and bicycle paths on certain roads. There were also proposals to support organizations that provide nighttime patrols and to strengthen support for Ukraine.

Other important items on the agenda included changes to the rules regarding compensation for politicians and the introduction of debates in the municipal council. Decisions were also made regarding new members for various committees, such as the Culture and Education Committee and the Technical Committee, following the departure of previous members.
